behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module handoff: three source clients -> aggregation/composition -> summary input payload. Unit is too low because a single-function test can miss wiring/field-drop bugs between modules (MOCK-MISS). Real OS/network/subprocess is not required to reproduce this specific failure; source adapters can be stubbed in-process (REAL-INFRA, MOCK-HIDE). No dependence on model judgment yet, only on payload composition (LLM-DEP false). Rejected higher levels: System is disqualified by behavior text focusing on "content passed" inclusion, not real HTTP/network behavior; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no claim about model output quality or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real collector/orchestrator modules with test doubles for the three source adapters returning distinct sentinel values; execute collection + handoff and assert the exact payload sent into summary generation contains all three sections/keys and values.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is resilience/state-propagation across modules (source error -> section fallback marker -> report still produced), so Unit is too low for the real failure boundary (MOCK-MISS). In-process failure injection (e.g., one adapter raises/unavailable response) is sufficient; no real network/OS behavior is required by the behavior wording (REAL-INFRA). Mocking does not inherently hide this fallback bug if wiring is real at module level (MOCK-HIDE). No model-quality dependence and no multi-agent chain (LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP false). Rejected higher levels: System disqualified because behavior says "unavailable" generically, not real timeout/socket semantics; Agentic/Workflow disqualified because correctness criterion is fallback handling, not model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with two source adapters returning data and one adapter failing; verify final report is still generated, includes `data unavailable` only for failed section, and process does not abort.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on generation quality/faithfulness of the summary itself, which is model output content (LLM-DEP true). Deterministic stubs would always pass and cannot detect hallucination/omission/misstatement (deterministic-assertion trap; MOCK-HIDE). Not Workflow because this behavior describes one agentic decision boundary (single summary generation), not multiple agentic stages in sequence (MULTI-STEP false). Rejected lower levels: Integration is disqualified because it can only validate routing/payload mechanics, not real summary accuracy.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative real input datasets into the actual summary-generation model and score whether produced narrative preserves required key values/facts (with rubric or factual extraction checks) across repeated runs/tolerance bands.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is end-to-end infrastructure: real service connectivity and actual email delivery/inbox receipt (REAL-INFRA true). In-process mocks can hide transport/auth/protocol/inbox-routing failures (MOCK-HIDE). Agentic is not required by this specific acceptance criterion because it checks successful end-to-end delivery path, not model-output quality; summary content can be fixed/minimally validated while still testing the real delivery pipeline. Workflow is disqualified because behavior does not require validating multiple model decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled/report runner against real reachable test endpoints (or staging equivalents) and a real mailbox, then assert one full report message is received by configured recipient with expected subject/body structure and run metadata.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is module orchestration/state persistence: delivery failure -> local persistence -> subsequent run retry consumption. This can be reproduced with real local filesystem plus injected delivery failure in-process; no real OS scheduler daemon or real network outage is required by the behavior text (REAL-INFRA checked, System rejected). Unit is too low because cross-run/state handoff across components is central (MOCK-MISS). No dependence on model judgment and no multi-agent chain (LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP false). Rejected higher level: System disqualified because behavior does not require validating real SMTP/network stack behavior, only unreachable-condition handling and retry semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report job twice in a controlled test environment: first run forces email sender failure and asserts fallback file is written; second run simulates sender recovery and asserts persisted report is retried/sent and fallback state is cleared or marked delivered.
